title: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Southern District Council (markets) Bye-laws
title_en: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Southern District Council (markets) Bye-laws

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Bye-laws may be cited as the Southern District Council (Markets) Bye-laws.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Establishment of markets
provisions.content:
3. Establishment of markets
(1) The Council may establish markets within the council area as it considers necessary.
(2) A market shall be under the control and supervision of a market master.

provisions.title: Permit to operate stall in market
provisions.content:
4. Permit to operate stall in market
(1) A person shall not operate any stall in a market within the council area without a permit issued under these Bye-laws.

definitions.definition: the area within the jurisdiction of the Council
a health inspector employed by the Government or by the Council
a market established by the Council under these Bye-laws
a person appointed by the Council to be a market master for the purpose of these Bye-laws
any building, stand, shelter, table, place or plot within a market set aside for the sale of merchandise or for the provision of a service to the public
a person by whom a stall is hired under these Bye-laws.
